sentences.sentence: Playing is a serious business.
sentences.translation: 玩耍是一件严肃的事。

sentences.sentence: Children engrossed in a make-believe world, fox cubs play-fighting or kittens teasing a ball of string aren't just having fun.
sentences.translation: 沉浸在想象世界的孩子、互相嬉闹的小狐和逗弄毛线球的小猫，并不只是玩得开心。

sentences.sentence: Play may look like a carefree and exuberant way to pass the time before the hard work of adulthood comes along, but there's much more to it than that.
sentences.translation: 玩耍看似是在成年艰苦生活到来前轻松而兴奋地打发时间，但它远不止如此。

structure.text: Play may look like a carefree and exuberant way to pass the time before the hard work of adulthood comes along, but there's much more to it than that.

sentences.sentence: For a start, play can even cost animals their lives.

sentences.sentence: Eighty per cent of deaths among juvenile fur seals occur because playing pups fail to spot predators approaching.
sentences.translation: 幼年海狗的死亡中有八成发生，是因为玩耍的幼崽没有发现靠近的捕食者。

sentences.sentence: It is also extremely expensive in terms of energy.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。

sentences.sentence: Playful young animals use around two or three per cent of their energy cavorting, and in children that figure can be closer to fifteen per cent. "Even two or three per cent is huge," says John Byers of Idaho University. "You just don't find animals wasting energy like that," he adds.
sentences.translation: 活泼的幼兽会用大约百分之二到三的能量嬉戏，儿童这一比例可接近百分之十五。

sentences.sentence: There must be a reason.

sentences.sentence: But if play is not simply a developmental hiccup, as biologists once thought, why did it evolve?

sentences.sentence: Playfulness, it seems, is common only among mammals, although a few of the larger-brained birds also indulge.

sentences.sentence: Animals at play often use unique signs - tail-wagging in dogs, for example - to indicate that activity superficially resembling adult behavior is not really in earnest.
sentences.translation: 玩耍时，动物常使用独特信号——例如狗摇尾巴——表明看起来像成年行为的活动其实并非认真进行。

sentences.sentence: A popular explanation of play has been that it helps juveniles develop the skills they will need to hunt, mate and socialise as adults.
sentences.translation: 一种流行解释认为，玩耍帮助幼体发展成年后狩猎、交配和社交所需的技能。

sentences.sentence: Another has been that it allows young animals to get in shape for adult life by improving their respiratory endurance.

sentences.sentence: If play evolved to build muscle or as a kind of endurance training, then you would expect to see permanent benefits.

sentences.sentence: But Byers points out that the benefits of increased exercise disappear rapidly after training stops, so any improvement in endurance resulting from juvenile play would be lost by adulthood. "If the function of play was to get into shape," says Byers, "the optimum time for playing would depend on when it was most advantageous for the young of a particular species to do so.
sentences.translation: 但拜尔斯指出，锻炼停止后增强运动带来的好处很快消失，因此幼年玩耍带来的耐力提升到成年时会丧失。 拜尔斯说：“若玩耍的功能是塑形，那么最佳玩耍时间取决于某个物种幼体何时最适合这样做。 但实际并非如此。”

structure.text: But Byers points out that the benefits of increased exercise disappear rapidly after training stops, so any improvement in endurance resulting from juvenile play would be lost by adulthood. "If the function of play was to get into shape," says Byers, "the optimum time for playing would depend on when it was most advantageous for the young of a particular species to do so.

sentences.sentence: In one study, behavioural ecologist Tim Caro, from the University of California, looked at the predatory play of kittens and their predatory behaviour when they reached adulthood.
sentences.translation: 加州大学的行为生态学家蒂姆·卡罗在一项研究中，考察了小猫的捕食性玩耍以及它们成年后的捕食行为。

sentences.sentence: He found that the way the cats played had no significant effect on their hunting prowess in later life.

sentences.sentence: Earlier this year, Sergio Pellis of Lethbridge University, Canada, reported that there is a strong positive link between brain size and playfulness among mammals in general.
sentences.translation: 今年早些时候，加拿大莱斯布里奇大学的塞尔焦·佩利斯报告说，哺乳动物整体的脑大小与爱玩程度之间有很强的正相关。

sentences.sentence: Comparing measurements for fifteen orders of mammal, he and his team found larger brains (for a given body size) are linked to greater playfulness.
sentences.translation: 他和团队比较了十五个哺乳动物目的数据，发现按相同体型计，更大的大脑与更强的玩耍性相关。

sentences.sentence: The converse was also found to be true.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。
注意本句的介词短语和非谓语成分不构成新的主句。

sentences.sentence: Robert Barton of Durham University believes that, because large brains are more sensitive to developmental stimuli than smaller brains, they require more play to help mould them for adulthood. “I concluded it's to do with learning, and with the importance of environmental data to the brain during development,” he says.
sentences.translation: 达勒姆大学的罗伯特·巴顿认为，大脑越大，对发育刺激越敏感，因此比小脑更需要玩耍来塑造成年状态。 他说：“我的结论是，这与学习有关，也与发育时环境数据对大脑的重要性有关。”

structure.text: Robert Barton of Durham University believes that, because large brains are more sensitive to developmental stimuli than smaller brains, they require more play to help mould them for adulthood. “I concluded it's to do with learning, and with the importance of environmental data to the brain during development,” he says.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。
注意本句的介词短语和非谓语成分不构成新的主句。

sentences.sentence: According to Byers, the timing of the playful stage in young animals provides an important clue to what's going on.
sentences.translation: 拜尔斯认为，幼兽玩耍阶段出现的时间为理解这一过程提供了重要线索。

structure.text: According to Byers, the timing of the playful stage in young animals provides an important clue to what's going on.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。
注意本句的介词短语和非谓语成分不构成新的主句。

sentences.sentence: If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development, you discover a pattern typically associated with a 'sensitive period' - a brief development window during which the brain can actually be modified in ways that are not possible earlier or later in life.
sentences.translation: 如果绘制幼体在发育中每天投入玩耍的时间，会发现一种通常与“敏感期”相关的模式：在这个短暂的发育窗口，大脑会以生命早晚阶段无法实现的方式真正被改变。

structure.text: If you plot the amount of time a juvenile devotes to play each day over the course of its development, you discover a pattern typically associated with a 'sensitive period' - a brief development window during which the brain can actually be modified in ways that are not possible earlier or later in life.

sentences.sentence: Other researchers have found that play in cats, rats and mice is at its most intense just as this "window of opportunity" reaches its peak.

sentences.sentence: "People have not paid enough attention to the amount of the brain activated by play," says Marc Bekoff from Colorado University.
sentences.translation: 科罗拉多大学的马克·贝科夫说：“人们没有足够重视玩耍激活的大脑范围。”

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。
注意本句的介词短语和非谓语成分不构成新的主句。

sentences.sentence: Bekoff studied coyote pups at play and found that the kind of behaviour involved was markedly more variable and unpredictable than that of adults.
sentences.translation: 贝科夫研究了玩耍的郊狼幼崽，发现其中的行为比成年动物的行为明显更多变、更不可预测。

structure.text: Bekoff studied coyote pups at play and found that the kind of behaviour involved was markedly more variable and unpredictable than that of adults.

sentences.sentence: Such behaviour activates many different parts of the brain, he reasons.
sentences.translation: 他认为，这类行为会激活大脑的许多不同部分。

structure.text: Such behaviour activates many different parts of the brain, he reasons.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。
注意本句的介词短语和非谓语成分不构成新的主句。

sentences.sentence: Bekoff likens it to a behavioural kaleidoscope, with animals at play jumping rapidly between activities. "They use behaviour from a lot of different contexts - predation, aggression, reproduction," he says. "Their developing brain is getting all sorts of stimulation.
sentences.translation: 贝科夫把它比作行为万花筒：玩耍的动物会在活动之间迅速转换。 他说：“它们使用来自许多不同情境的行为——捕食、攻击、繁殖。” 他说：“它们正在发育的大脑得到各种刺激。

structure.text: Bekoff likens it to a behavioural kaleidoscope, with animals at play jumping rapidly between activities. "They use behaviour from a lot of different contexts - predation, aggression, reproduction," he says. "Their developing brain is getting all sorts of stimulation.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。
注意本句的介词短语和非谓语成分不构成新的主句。

sentences.sentence: Not only is more of the brain involved in play than was suspected, but it also seems to activate higher cognitive processes. "There's enormous cognitive involvement in play", says Bekoff.
sentences.translation: 玩耍涉及的大脑区域不仅比原先猜想的更多，它似乎还会激活更高层次的认知过程。 贝科夫说：“玩耍中有巨大的认知投入。”

structure.text: Not only is more of the brain involved in play than was suspected, but it also seems to activate higher cognitive processes. "There's enormous cognitive involvement in play", says Bekoff.

difficult_points: 先确定句子的主干，再把修饰成分分层理解，避免逐词硬译。

sentences.sentence: He points out that play often involves complex assessments of playmates, ideas of reciprocity and the use of specialised signals and rules.
sentences.translation: 他指出，玩耍常涉及对玩伴的复杂评估、互惠观念以及专门信号和规则的使用。

sentences.sentence: He believes that play creates a brain that has greater behavioural flexibility and improved potential for learning later in life.
sentences.translation: 他相信，玩耍造就了行为更灵活、日后学习潜力更强的大脑。

sentences.sentence: We already know that rat pups denied the chance to play grow smaller brain components and fail to develop the ability to apply social rules when they interact with their peers.
sentences.translation: 我们已经知道，被剥夺玩耍机会的鼠崽，大脑组成部分会变小，也无法在与同伴互动时形成运用社会规则的能力。
